description: Rythu Bandhu is an investment support scheme that provides financial assistance to land-owning farmers of Telangana before each crop season to help them purchase seeds, fertilizers, pesticides and other agricultural inputs without depending on moneylenders.
eligibility: Must be a resident of Telangana state
Must be a land-owning farmer (pattadar)
Land must be registered in the farmer's name in revenue records
Both wet land and dry land farmers are eligible
Tenant farmers are NOT eligible under this scheme
benefits: ₹10,000 per acre per year (₹5,000 per crop season)
Two installments — Kharif season and Rabi season
Amount directly credited to farmer's bank account (DBT)
how_to_apply: Farmers are auto-enrolled based on land records. Visit nearest Agriculture Office or MeeSeva center if not enrolled.

description: Rythu Bharosa is a comprehensive farmer support scheme combining PM-KISAN benefits with state government assistance, providing income support to all eligible farmer families in Telangana.
eligibility: Resident farmer of Telangana
Small and marginal farmers (land up to 2 acres)
Must have valid land records
Family income should be below taxable limit
benefits: Access to subsidized seeds and fertilizers
how_to_apply: Register at nearest Agriculture Extension Office or apply online at rythu.telangana.gov.in

description: Rythu Bima is a group life insurance scheme for farmers in Telangana, providing financial security to farmer families in case of the farmer's untimely death due to any reason including accidents.
eligibility: Farmers aged between 18 and 59 years
Must be enrolled in Rythu Bandhu scheme
Registered pattadar (land owner) in Telangana
Premium is fully paid by the state government
benefits: ₹5 lakh life insurance coverage per farmer
Coverage for death due to natural causes and accidents
Premium paid entirely by Telangana government — farmer pays nothing
how_to_apply: Auto-enrolled with Rythu Bandhu. For claims, approach nearest Agriculture Office with death certificate and nominee documents.

description: Kalyana Lakshmi provides one-time financial assistance to families belonging to SC, ST, BC and EWS categories for the marriage of their daughters, reducing the financial burden on economically weaker families.
eligibility: Bride must be a resident of Telangana
Bride must be 18 years or above at the time of marriage
Family must belong to SC, ST, BC-A, BC-B, BC-C, BC-D or EWS category
Annual family income must be below ₹2 lakh
Applicable only for first marriage
Application must be submitted within 3 years of marriage
benefits: ₹1,00,116 one-time financial assistance (as of latest revision)
Amount credited directly to bride's bank account
Can be used for marriage expenses
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of bride and parents
Age proof (birth certificate or 10th marks memo)
how_to_apply: Apply online at telanganaepass.cgg.gov.in or visit nearest MeeSeva center.

description: Shaadi Mubarak provides financial assistance for the marriage of daughters from Muslim and other minority communities in Telangana, similar to Kalyana Lakshmi but specifically for minority families.
eligibility: Bride must belong to Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi or Jain community
Bride must be 18 years or above
Resident of Telangana
Annual family income below ₹2 lakh
First marriage only
benefits: Directly credited to bride's bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at Minority Welfare Office or online at telanganaepass.cgg.gov.in

description: Aasara Pension is a monthly financial assistance scheme for vulnerable sections of society including senior citizens, widows, disabled persons, toddy tappers, weavers and other eligible categories who have no means of income.
eligibility: Senior citizens aged 57 years and above with no income
Widows of any age with no income support
Persons with disability (40% or more) with no income
Persons suffering from AIDS
Toddy tappers with no other income
Weavers with no other income
Annual family income must be below ₹1.5 lakh
Must be a resident of Telangana
benefits: Higher pension for specific categories like AIDS patients
Amount directly credited to bank account or paid at doorstep
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat office (rural) or Ward office (urban). Verification done by Revenue officials.

description: Aarogya Lakshmi provides free nutritious meals to pregnant women, lactating mothers and children below 6 years at Anganwadi centers to reduce malnutrition and improve maternal and child health.
eligibility: Pregnant women (from 3rd month of pregnancy)
Lactating mothers (up to 6 months after delivery)
Children aged 6 months to 6 years
Must be registered at local Anganwadi center
Beneficiaries from BPL families are prioritized
benefits: Free nutritious meal every day at Anganwadi center
documents_required: Pregnancy confirmation from doctor / health worker
how_to_apply: Register at nearest Anganwadi center with pregnancy confirmation and Aadhaar card.

description: KCR Kit encourages institutional deliveries by providing a kit of essential baby care items and cash assistance to mothers who deliver in government hospitals, improving mother and newborn health.
eligibility: Pregnant women delivering in government hospitals or government-aided hospitals
Must be a resident of Telangana
Applicable for first two live births only
benefits: Baby care kit worth ₹12,000 (includes clothes, toys, oil, soap, diapers etc.)
Total benefit: ₹25,000 for girl child, ₹24,000 for boy child
how_to_apply: No separate application needed. Register at government hospital during delivery and hospital staff will process the KCR Kit.

description: Dalit Bandhu is a flagship scheme to economically empower Dalit (SC) families by providing them direct financial assistance to start their own businesses and enterprises, breaking the cycle of poverty.
eligibility: Must belong to Scheduled Caste (SC) community
Resident of Telangana
One beneficiary per family
Priority to landless SC laborers
Annual income below ₹2.5 lakh
benefits: ₹10 lakh direct financial assistance per family
Can be used to start any business or enterprise of choice
No repayment required — it is a grant, not a loan
how_to_apply: Apply through Village/Ward Secretariat or District Collector office. Selection through local committees.

description: Gruha Lakshmi (also known as 2BHK scheme) provides free double bedroom houses to homeless and economically weaker section families in Telangana, addressing the housing shortage for the poor.
eligibility: Must be a resident of Telangana for at least 10 years
Must not own any house or residential plot
Annual family income below ₹3 lakh
Priority to SC, ST, BC, minorities and EWS families
Single women, widows and disabled persons get priority
benefits: Free 2BHK house (560 sq ft built-up area)
House registered in woman's name (wife or female head of family)
Basic amenities — electricity connection, water connection
documents_required: Declaration that applicant owns no house
how_to_apply: Apply at Village/Ward Secretariat or online at housing.telangana.gov.in

description: Indiramma Housing provides financial assistance to economically weaker section families to construct or repair their own houses, ensuring safe and permanent shelter for the poor.
eligibility: Economically weaker section families
Annual income below ₹1.5 lakh
Must own a plot of land
Should not have availed any other government housing scheme
Resident of Telangana
benefits: Up to ₹2 lakh financial assistance for house construction
Amount released in installments based on construction progress
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Ward office.

description: Telangana State Industrial Project Approval and Self-Certification System provides single-window clearance for setting up businesses and industries, promoting self-employment and entrepreneurship.
eligibility: Any citizen of India wishing to start a business in Telangana
Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)
Startups and entrepreneurs
benefits: Single window clearance for all business approvals
Subsidies and incentives for new industries
how_to_apply: Apply online at ipass.telangana.gov.in

description: Full fee reimbursement for SC and ST students pursuing higher education in recognized colleges and universities in Telangana, ensuring no financial barrier prevents education.
eligibility: Must belong to SC or ST community
Resident of Telangana
Pursuing graduation, post-graduation or professional courses (Engineering, Medicine, Law etc.)
Enrolled in a recognized government or private college
Annual family income below ₹2.5 lakh (for private colleges)
No income limit for government college students
benefits: Covers Engineering, Medical, Law, MBA, MCA and other professional courses
how_to_apply: Apply online at telanganaepass.cgg.gov.in. College principal verifies and forwards application.

description: Free residential schooling and coaching for BC (Backward Class) students to improve educational outcomes and prepare them for competitive examinations.
eligibility: Students belonging to BC-A, BC-B, BC-C, BC-D categories
Resident of Telangana
Students seeking admission to BC residential schools
Annual family income below ₹2 lakh
benefits: Free residential schooling from Class 5 to Class 12
Free coaching for competitive exams (EAMCET, JEE, NEET)
how_to_apply: Apply at BC Welfare Department or District BC Welfare Officer during admission season.

description: Financial scholarship assistance for BC/OBC students pursuing post-matriculation education including graduation, post-graduation and professional courses.
eligibility: Must belong to BC (OBC) category
Pursuing post-matric courses (after 10th standard)
Resident of Telangana
Annual family income below ₹1 lakh (for state scholarship)
benefits: Maintenance allowance per month during course
Thesis typing charges for research scholars
how_to_apply: Apply online at telanganaepass.cgg.gov.in

description: Kanti Velugu is a comprehensive eye care program providing free eye screening and vision correction to all residents of Telangana, especially targeting school children and the elderly.
eligibility: All residents of Telangana — no income restriction
School children (Class 1 to Class 10) get priority
Senior citizens
Any person with vision problems
benefits: Free eye screening and vision test
Free cataract surgery for those who need it
Free treatment for other eye conditions
how_to_apply: Eye screening camps are organized at schools, gram panchayats and public places. No application needed — attend the camp.

description: Aarogyasri provides cashless health insurance coverage for serious illnesses and major surgeries to BPL families and other eligible categories in Telangana at empanelled hospitals.
eligibility: White ration card holders (BPL families)
Annual family income below ₹5 lakh
Resident of Telangana
Journalists registered with state government
Auto drivers with valid license
benefits: Cashless treatment up to ₹5 lakh per year per family
Treatment at empanelled government and private hospitals
Covers pre and post hospitalization expenses
Free transportation to hospital in some cases
how_to_apply: Obtain Aarogyasri card from Village/Ward Secretariat. At the hospital, show Aadhaar and ration card at Aarogyasri help desk.

description: This scheme supports shepherd and Golla/Kuruma communities by distributing sheep units to help them establish sustainable livelihoods through animal husbandry.
eligibility: Must belong to shepherd community (Golla, Kuruma, Yadav)
Resident of Telangana
Should not have availed scheme before
Annual income below ₹1.5 lakh
Age between 18 and 50 years
how_to_apply: Apply at District Animal Husbandry Office.

description: Free residential education for ST (Scheduled Tribe) students from Class 5 to Class 12 with quality schooling, food, accommodation and coaching for competitive examinations.
eligibility: Must belong to Scheduled Tribe (ST) community
Resident of Telangana
Seeking admission to Class 5 or higher
Annual family income preferably below ₹2 lakh
benefits: Free coaching for EAMCET, JEE, NEET
documents_required: Previous class TC and marks memo
how_to_apply: Apply at Tribal Welfare Residential School during admission season or at District Tribal Welfare Office.

description: Mahalakshmi scheme provides direct financial assistance and support to women-headed households and economically vulnerable women to improve their livelihood and financial independence.
eligibility: Women-headed household
Widow, divorced or separated women
Annual family income below ₹2 lakh
Resident of Telangana
Age 18 years and above
benefits: Free bus pass for TSRTC buses
how_to_apply: Apply at Village/Ward Secretariat or Women & Child Welfare office.

scheme_name: DWCRA (Development of Women and Children in Rural Areas)
description: DWCRA promotes self-employment and income generation for rural women through self-help groups (SHGs), providing them credit, training and marketing support.
eligibility: Rural women in Telangana
BPL or low-income families
Willing to form or join a Self Help Group (SHG)
Age 18 to 60 years
how_to_apply: Contact local MEPMA (Mission for Elimination of Poverty in Municipal Areas) or SERP office in rural areas.

description: Monthly financial assistance and various support schemes for persons with disabilities in Telangana to help them meet basic needs and lead a dignified life.
eligibility: Must have disability of 40% or more
Must possess a valid disability certificate (UDID card)
Resident of Telangana
Annual family income below ₹1.5 lakh
Not employed in government service
benefits: Monthly pension ₹1,500 to ₹3,000 depending on disability type
Subsidy on assistive devices (wheelchair, hearing aid, crutches)
documents_required: UDID card (disability certificate from government hospital)
how_to_apply: Apply at District Disabled Welfare Officer or Village/Ward Secretariat.

description: Social security benefits including pension, insurance and welfare support for unorganised sector workers registered under the Building & Other Construction Workers Act and e-Shram portal.
eligibility: Workers in unorganised sector (construction, weaving, fishing, domestic work, street vending etc.)
Aged between 18 and 60 years
Must register on e-Shram portal
Annual income below ₹2.5 lakh
Not a member of EPFO or ESIC
documents_required: Aadhaar Card linked to mobile number
how_to_apply: Register at eshram.gov.in online or at nearest CSC (Common Service Centre). Labour card from Telangana Labour Department office.

description: Central government scheme implemented in Telangana to provide pucca houses to homeless and houseless families in rural areas with basic amenities.
eligibility: Houseless families or families living in zero or one-room kutcha houses
Rural residents of Telangana
SC, ST, minorities and EWS families get priority
Widows and next-of-kin of defence personnel killed in action
Persons with disabilities
Not availed any previous housing scheme
benefits: ₹1.2 lakh financial assistance in plain areas
Free toilet under Swachh Bharat Mission
how_to_apply: Beneficiaries are selected based on SECC (Socio Economic Caste Census) data. Contact Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office.

description: Central government pension scheme for destitute senior citizens above 60 years with no income support, implemented through the state government in Telangana.
eligibility: Age 60 years and above
Must be destitute — no regular income or financial support from family
Annual income below ₹1 lakh
Resident of Telangana
BPL status preferred
benefits: ₹200 per month (central share) + state top-up making total ₹2,016 per month
For age 80 and above — ₹500 per month central share
Amount directly credited to bank account
documents_required: Age proof (birth certificate, school certificate, voter ID)
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Ward Secretariat. Verification by Revenue official.
